nodes.label: Work Order Submit API Tests  Tests for the work order submit functionality as de
Raised when an invalid state transition is attempted.
State machine for work order status transitions.
Check if a transition is valid.
Get the next state for a given transition.
Execute a state transition on a work order.
Mock repository for work order data access.
Create a new work order in draft status.
Check if user has a specific role.
Request schema for creating a work order.
Service layer for work order operations.
Create a new work order in draft status.
Submit a draft work order for approval.                  Business rules:
Reject a pending work order with reason.
Create a fresh mock repository for each test.
Create a fresh state machine for each test.
Create a fresh notification publisher for each test.
Create a mock user service for each test.
Create a work order service with mocked dependencies.
Sample valid work order creation data.
ATB-1.1: Create work order returns HTTP 201 with valid data.
Create work order with valid data should return HTTP 201.                  Expec
Newly created work order should have DRAFT status.
Newly created work order should have version=1 for optimistic locking.
ATB-1.2: Create work order with title exceeding 200 characters should return HTT
Create work order with title > 200 characters should return HTTP 422.
Create work order with title exactly 200 characters should succeed.
Create work order with empty title should return HTTP 422.
ATB-1.3: Submit draft work order should transition to PENDING_APPROVAL status.
Submit a draft work order should change status from DRAFT to PENDING_APPROVAL.
Submit should trigger notify_approver message to Redis Stream.
Only the creator should be able to submit their work order.
Submit should update the work order's updated_at timestamp.
ATB-1.4: Submitting an already submitted work order should return HTTP 400.
Submitting a work order that is already in PENDING_APPROVAL status should fail.
Submitting an approved work order should return HTTP 400.
Submitting a rejected work order should return HTTP 400.
Test the state machine transitions directly.
State machine should allow DRAFT -> PENDING_APPROVAL transition.
State machine should reject DRAFT -> APPROVED (skipping approval).
Approved state should not allow any outgoing transitions.
State machine should record transition history.
Test edge cases for work order submission.
Submitting a non-existent work order should raise ValueError.
Submitting with invalid work order ID should raise appropriate error.
Notification publishing should not block the main flow.
Test complete work order lifecycle from creation to approval.
Test complete lifecycle: DRAFT -> PENDING_APPROVAL -> APPROVED.
Test complete lifecycle: DRAFT -> PENDING_APPROVAL -> REJECTED.
Notifications should be triggered in correct sequence for full lifecycle.
